title: ranunculus hispidus var. typicus
intro: Ranunculus hispidus var. typicus, commonly known as rough-leaved buttercup, is a perennial plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) that grows in woodland areas and along streams. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus hispidus var. typicus include reducing pain and swelling, as well as promoting tissue repair, making it a potential treatment for various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in water or using them in tincture form to create a topical remedy for skin issues.
